input.question:
We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Please list all rules relevant to `Scatter Shields`. Answer with only the rule numbers (i.e.: AA.1.1.1) separated by commas and no other words.



The rules relevant to `Scatter Shields` are:

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.5.2.7
            TITLE: chain drive - scatter shields for chains must:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.2.7: Chain Drive - Scatter shields for chains must:
T.5.2.7 Chain Drive - Scatter shields for chains must: 
a. Be made of 2.66 mm (0.105 inch) minimum thickness steel (no alternatives are allowed)
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 67 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
b. Have a minimum width equal to three times the width of the chain 
c. Be centered on the center line of the chain  
d. Stay aligned with the chain under all conditions

RULE: t.5.2.5
            TITLE: scatter shields may be composed of multiple pieces.  any gaps must be small (< 3 mm)

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.2.5: Scatter shields may be composed of multiple pieces.  Any gaps must be small (< 3 mm)
T.5.2.5 Scatter shields may be composed of multiple pieces.  Any gaps must be small (< 3 mm)

RULE: t.5.3.3
            TITLE: the motor scatter shield must be:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.3.3: The Motor Scatter Shield must be:
T.5.3.3 The Motor Scatter Shield must be: 
• Made from aluminum alloy 6061-T6 or steel 
• Minimum thickness:  1.0 mm

RULE: t.5.3.2
            TITLE: a scatter shield must be included around the motor(s) when one or both:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.3.2: A Scatter Shield must be included around the Motor(s) when one or both:
T.5.3.2 A Scatter Shield must be included around the Motor(s) when one or both: 
• The motor casing rotates around the stator 
• The motor case is perforated

RULE: t.5.2.8
            TITLE: non-metallic belt drive - scatter shields for belts must:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.2.8: Non-metallic Belt Drive - Scatter shields for belts must:
T.5.2.8 Non-metallic Belt Drive - Scatter shields for belts must: 
a. Be made from 3.0 mm minimum thickness aluminum alloy 6061-T6 
b. Have a minimum width that is equal to 1.7 times the width of the belt.    
c. Be centered on the center line of the belt  
d. Stay aligned with the belt under all conditions

RULE: t.3.1.8
            TITLE: the brake system must be protected with scatter shields from failure of the drive train (see

            CONTENT:
            rule T.3.1.8: The Brake System must be protected with scatter shields from failure of the drive train (see
T.3.1.8 The Brake System must be protected with scatter shields from failure of the drive train (see 
T.5.2) or from minor collisions.

RULE: t.1.8.4
            TITLE: details

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.8.4: Details
T.1.8.4 Details 
a. Firewalls composed of multiple panels must overlap and be sealed at the joints 
Sealing between firewalls must not be a stressed part of the Firewall 
b. Grommets must be used to seal any pass through for wiring, cables, etc
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 58 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
c. Any seals or adhesives used with the Firewall must be rated for the application 
environment

RULE: t.1.9.1
            TITLE: tractive system firewalls must meet the requirements of t.1.8 above

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.9.1: Tractive System Firewalls must meet the requirements of T.1.8 above
T.1.9.1 Tractive System Firewalls must meet the requirements of T.1.8 above

RULE: t.1.3.3
            TITLE: all moving suspension and steering components and other sharp edges inside the cockpit

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.3.3: All moving suspension and steering components and other sharp edges inside the cockpit
T.1.3.3 All moving suspension and steering components and other sharp edges inside the cockpit 
between the Front Hoop and a vertical plane 100 mm rearward of the pedals must be covered 
by a shield made of a solid material.  
Moving components include, but are not limited to springs, shock absorbers, rocker arms, anti-
roll/sway bars, steering racks and steering column CV joints.

RULE: t.1.8.3
            TITLE: positioning

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.8.3: Positioning
T.1.8.3 Positioning  
The Firewall must extend sufficiently far upwards and/or rearwards and/or sideways where 
any point on the drivers body less than 100 mm above the bottom of the helmet of the tallest 
driver must not be in direct line of sight with any part given in T.1.8.1 above

RULE: t.5.2.1
            TITLE: exposed high speed final drivetrain equipment such as continuously variable transmissions

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.2.1: Exposed high speed final drivetrain equipment such as Continuously Variable Transmissions
T.5.2.1 Exposed high speed final drivetrain equipment such as Continuously Variable Transmissions 
(CVTs), sprockets, gears, pulleys, torque converters, clutches, belt drives, clutch drives and 
electric motors, must be fitted with scatter shields intended to contain drivetrain parts in case 
of radial failure

RULE: t.5.2.9
            TITLE: attachment fasteners - all fasteners attaching scatter shields and guards must be 6 mm or

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.2.9: Attachment Fasteners - All fasteners attaching scatter shields and guards must be 6 mm or
T.5.2.9 Attachment Fasteners - All fasteners attaching scatter shields and guards must be 6 mm or 
1/4” minimum diameter Critical Fasteners, see T.8.2

RULE: t.5.2.4
            TITLE: frame members or existing components that exceed the scatter shield material requirements

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.2.4: Frame Members or existing components that exceed the scatter shield material requirements
T.5.2.4 Frame Members or existing components that exceed the scatter shield material requirements 
may be used as part of the shield.

RULE: t.5.2.6
            TITLE: if equipped, the engine drive sprocket cover may be used as part of the scatter shield system.

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.2.6: If equipped, the engine drive sprocket cover may be used as part of the scatter shield system.
T.5.2.6 If equipped, the engine drive sprocket cover may be used as part of the scatter shield system.

RULE: t.6.2.2
            TITLE: the shields must be steel or aluminum with a minimum thickness of 1 mm.

            CONTENT:
            rule T.6.2.2: The shields must be steel or aluminum with a minimum thickness of 1 mm.
T.6.2.2 The shields must be steel or aluminum with a minimum thickness of 1 mm.

RULE: t.2.1
            TITLE: harness definitions

            CONTENT:
            rule T.2.1: Harness Definitions
T.2.1 Harness Definitions 
a. 5 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts and one Anti-Submarine 
Belt.  
b. 6 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts and two leg or Anti-
Submarine Belts.  
c. 7 Point Harness – consists of two Lap Belts, two Shoulder Belts, two leg or Anti-
Submarine Belts and a negative g or Z Belt.   
d. Upright Driving Position - with a seat back angled at 30° or less from the vertical as 
measured along the line joining the two 200 mm circles of the template of the 95th 
percentile male as defined in F.5.6.4 and positioned per F.5.6.5 
e. Reclined Driving Position - with a seat back angled at more than 30° from the vertical as 
measured along the line joining the two 200 mm circles of the template of the 95th 
percentile male as defined in F.5.6.4 and positioned per F.5.6.5 
f. Chest to Groin Line - the straight line that in side view follows the line of the Shoulder 
Belts from the chest to the release buckle.

RULE: ev.11.2.1
            TITLE: all participating team members must wear safety glasses with side shields at any time when:

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.11.2.1: All participating team members must wear safety glasses with side shields at any time when:
EV.11.2.1 All participating team members must wear safety glasses with side shields at any time when:  
a. Parts of the Tractive System are exposed while energized  
b. Work is performed on the Accumulators
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 108 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023

RULE: f.11.2.2
            TITLE: rear impact protection

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.2.2: Rear Impact Protection
F.11.2.2 Rear Impact Protection 
a. All Accumulator Containers must be protected from rear impact:  
• When the rear impact structure is 100 mm or less from an Accumulator Container, 
the structure must be Equivalent to Side Impact Structure (F.6.4, F.7.5)  
• When the rear impact structure is more than 100 mm from an Accumulator 
Container, the structure must meet  F.5.16 Component Protection 
• The Accumulator Container must not be part of the Equivalent structure. 
b. The Rear Impact Protection for the Accumulator Container must: 
• Go to the Upper Side Impact Height at minimum
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 53 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
• Have a structural and triangulated load path from the top of the Rear Impact 
Protection to the Upper Side Impact Structure  F.6.4, F.7.5 at the Main Hoop  
• Have a structural and triangulated load path from the bottom of the Rear Impact 
Protection to the Lower Side Impact Structure  F.6.4, F.7.5 at the Main Hoop  
c. Other Tractive System components in a position below 350 mm from the ground must be 
protected from rear impact by structure meeting F.5.16 Component Protection

RULE: f.11.2.2
            TITLE: rear impact protection

            CONTENT:
            rule F.11.2.2: Rear Impact Protection
c. Other Tractive System components in a position below 350 mm from the ground must be 
protected from rear impact by structure meeting F.5.16 Component Protection 
d. Differential mounts or a plate behind upper and lower rear impact tubes may be used as 
Rear Impact Protection.   
If used, the mounts or plate must be 25 mm or more larger at both the top and bottom 
of the upper and lower tubes

RULE: t.1.1.3
            TITLE: fore and aft translation of the template is permitted during insertion.

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.1.3: Fore and aft translation of the template is permitted during insertion.
T.1.1.3 Fore and aft translation of the template is permitted during insertion.
